title: Inviting Algerian Egg Frittata
description: A floral, pillowy frittata that feels like a solo dinner with a touch of Algerian spice.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a 9‑inch non‑stick skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Sprinkle paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper; stir and cook 1 minute.
Whisk eggs in a bowl, add lemon zest and mint, then pour into the skillet.
Cook without stirring for 2 minutes until the edges begin to set.
Gently lift the edges with a spatula, tilt the pan, and let the uncooked egg flow underneath.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until the eggs are fully set, about 4 minutes.
Flip the frittata onto a plate, slide it back into the skillet, and cook the other side for 1 minute.
Garnish with chopped parsley, slice, and serve hot.
